mechanism_narrative: The elevated unemployment rate of 5.8% indicates persistent labor market slack. This slack restrains wage growth and aggregate demand, exerting downward pressure on long-term interest rates over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day yield change is positive in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE is above 5.5%, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls exceeding 139.5 million signal a tight labor market with upward pressure on wages and inflation. This expectation of higher inflation drives 10-year yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS above threshold leads to negative yield changes in more than 50% of cases over a 2-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero fed funds (0.09%) and elevated CPI (above 230) creates negative real interest rates. This distorts savings behavior and boosts inflation expectations, causing yields to rise over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the yield does not rise in at least 60% of instances when both conditions hold over a 3-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In the current regime of very low federal funds rates (0.09%), an increase in housing starts above 1,000,000 units signals strong housing demand and economic vigor. This optimism lifts expectations for future growth and inflation, raising 10-year yields over the next 60 trading days. The effect is weaker or reversed when fed funds are above 2%.
failure_condition: If yield responds negatively in more than 40% of instances when both regime and trigger are active over a 5-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: The level of industrial production above 105 indicates solid manufacturing activity. This supports ongoing economic expansion, which tends to be associated with rising long-term yields over the next 5 trading days via momentum in growth expectations.
failure_condition: If over any 6-month period, INDPRO > 105 is accompanied by falling yields in more than half of the observations, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales above $444 billion signal strong consumer spending, a key driver of inflation. Elevated inflation expectations push 10-year yields higher over the next 60 trading days as markets price in tighter monetary policy.
failure_condition: If RSAFS above threshold leads to yield decline in more than 30% of cases over a 2-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP is high (above $16,000 billion) and unemployment is high (above 5.5%), it indicates a potentially stagflationary scenario. The combination of high output and high joblessness confounds typical monetary policy responses, leading to uncertainty that may push yields upward due to increased term premium.
failure_condition: If yield does not increase in at least two-thirds of such episodes over any 10-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the zero lower bound (0.09%), further conventional monetary easing is not possible. Any positive economic data will then lead to higher yields as the market prices in eventual policy normalization and improved growth outlook.
failure_condition: If yield declines in more than 50% of instances with FEDFUNDS at ZLB over a 3-year rolling window, reject.
